evidence: ProjectContext writes a mutable global snapshot to `window.__PROJECT_CONTEXT__` inside `useEffect`, explicitly documenting non-context fallback consumers (`src/shared/contexts/ProjectContext.tsx:68-78`).

Cross-module coordination also happens through app-wide events (`useAppEventListener('selectShotForAddition', ...)`) rather than explicit module interfaces (`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts:114-116`).

Legacy compatibility boundaries remain active with runtime deprecation enforcement on every call (`src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade.ts:14-41`, `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts:56-69`) and the blind packet shows these as high-import hubs.

Type boundary is broadened by a mixed-domain barrel (`generation + shot`) that downstream modules import from for shot-only needs (`src/types/generationAndShots.ts:1-11`, `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts:5`).

UI overlay open/close control patterns are repeatedly pushed through props across independent components (`InstallInstructionsModal`, `DescriptionModal`, `MediaTypeFilter`, `ShotActions`), indicating fragmented ownership of transient UI state.

Join-clips task creation keeps parallel canonical and legacy input contracts: `CanonicalJoinClipsTaskInput` in `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts` and a large mirrored `JoinClipsTaskCompatParams` in `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts`.

Legacy-path behavior is still actively exercised in tests (`src/shared/lib/tasks/__tests__/joinClips.test.ts` cases for `starting_video_path`, `intermediate_video_paths`, and `source_*` video-edit fields), indicating dual dependency paths are still maintained.

`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts` explicitly widens input to `TravelBetweenImagesTaskInput & Partial<LegacyTravelBetweenImagesTaskParams>`, preserving mixed modern+legacy dependency surfaces.

Media lightbox has reverse type dependencies where hooks import component-level types from `../VideoLightbox` while `VideoLightbox.tsx` imports those hooks (`useVideoLightboxRenderModel`, `useVideoLightboxController`), creating a cycle-prone module graph.

`OperationResult` is used with incompatible semantics: `checkRateLimit` always returns `operationSuccess(...)` (including degraded/runtime-failure paths) while sibling helpers like `parseJsonBody` and `triggerCostCalculation` return `operationFailure(...)` for failure paths.
`checkRateLimit` computes `retryAfter` without lower-bound clamping, then `rateLimitResponse` and shared response envelope writers propagate that raw value to headers/body.

Recoverable client-side helpers expose mixed contracts: boolean (`safePlay`), thrown typed errors + fallback returns (`fetchToolSettingsSupabase`), and `OperationResult` (`consumePendingJoinClips`, `loadClipDuration`).
`useLineageChain` accepts nullable IDs but uses non-null assertions in `queryKey` and `queryFn`, making the public signature looser than runtime assumptions.
Deprecated shim modules exist without sunset metadata or enforcement hooks: `src/shared/components/theme/buttonVariants.ts:1-8` and `src/shared/lib/toolConstants.ts:1-6`.

Legacy dual-shape adapters are still first-class in shared runtime contracts: `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts:32-95` and `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/legacyStructureVideo.ts:18-50`.

Historical field fallback chains remain active without deprecation policy ownership/removal targets, e.g. `src/shared/lib/mediaTypeHelpers.ts:26-34`, `src/shared/lib/taskIdHelpers.ts:27-32`, `src/shared/lib/galleryUtils.ts:43-73`.
Scoped mutations in src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts enforce project ownership before writes (ensureProjectScope + verifyGenerationScope + .eq('project_id', projectId) on update/delete).

Sibling write path in src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts deletes generations with .in('id', idsToDelete) and no project/user predicate after lookups that are also unscoped by project.
In the same mutation module, createGeneration inserts rows directly from caller-supplied projectId without the project-scope prevalidation used by update/delete/toggle paths.

findings.summary: Project state is coordinated through implicit global/event channels instead of explicit module contracts.
evidence: Project provider mutates `window.__PROJECT_CONTEXT__` as a structural fallback (`ProjectContext.tsx:68-78`).
Media gallery hook listens to a global typed event (`selectShotForAddition`) (`useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts:114-116`).
Both patterns create hidden dependencies that are difficult to trace and test across module boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Replace global fallback/event-bus coordination with a single explicit project/gallery state access surface (context + selector hooks or injected service) and restrict global/event use to integration edges only.

findings.summary: Deprecated compatibility facades still sit on high-traffic paths, keeping old/new boundaries merged.
evidence: `getLegacySupabaseClient()` remains callable and wraps runtime governance enforcement (`legacySupabaseFacade.ts:14-41`) with removal target June 30, 2026.
`reportRuntimeError` remains exported as a deprecated alias and still routes through enforcement + modern handler (`handleError.ts:56-69`).
Blind packet marks both files as top import hubs, so compatibility surfaces still have broad blast radius.
findings.suggestion: Execute a bounded migration: codemod callsites to canonical APIs, add lint/import restrictions for deprecated entrypoints, and keep temporary adapter modules isolated from core shared paths.

findings.summary: A combined generation+shot type barrel increases cross-domain coupling for consumers that only need one side.
evidence: `generationAndShots.ts` exports both generation and shot contracts in one canonical barrel (`generationAndShots.ts:1-11`).
Media gallery imports `Shot` from this mixed barrel (`useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts:5`) even though it is shot-focused logic.
This pattern makes module dependencies less domain-specific and widens the impact of changes in shared type boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Split canonical barrels by domain (`generationTypes`, `shotTypes`) and migrate consumers to the narrowest domain barrel or direct source type path.

findings.summary: Overlay open/close state contracts are duplicated across many components, indicating missing shared interaction boundary.
evidence: Install modal uses `open`/`onOpenChange` prop contract (`InstallInstructionsModal.tsx:6-8,40`).
Description modal uses parallel but differently named `isOpen`/`onClose` contract (`DescriptionModal.tsx:7-8,13`).
MediaTypeFilter exposes optional controlled `open` and `onOpenChange` plus `contentRef` (`MediaTypeFilter.tsx:18-20,44-45`).
ShotActions embeds additional selector open state callbacks in nested prop groups (`ShotActions.tsx:15-22,57-63`).
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared overlay controller convention/hook (consistent controlled/uncontrolled API and naming) and standardize modal/select/tooltip wrappers on that contract.

findings.summary: Task builders still depend on mixed canonical and legacy input contracts across core flows
evidence: `joinClipsCompatAdapter` defines a broad legacy+modern input bag (`JoinClipsTaskCompatParams`) and conditionally maps legacy fields to canonical inputs.
`buildTravelBetweenImagesPayload` accepts `TravelBetweenImagesTaskInput & Partial<LegacyTravelBetweenImagesTaskParams>`, which keeps legacy structure fields in the main builder surface.
`individualTravelSegment` still imports and applies `stripLegacyStructureParams`, showing legacy schema translation remains in hot-path task construction.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a strict canonical DTO boundary at task-entry points and move legacy parsing into isolated adapter modules scheduled for removal. Keep core builders (`joinClips`, `travelBetweenImages`, `individualTravelSegment`) accepting only canonical types; enforce with compile-time types and adapter-only tests.

findings.summary: Join-clips compatibility wrapper duplicates canonical schema, increasing drift risk
evidence: `JoinClipsSharedTaskParams` in `joinClips.ts` and `JoinClipsTaskCompatParams` in `joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts` repeat many of the same fields (`prompt`, `context_frame_count`, `gap_frame_count`, `loras`, `phase_config`, `tool_type`, etc.).
The compat adapter adds additional legacy alternatives (`starting_video_path`, `intermediate_video_paths`, `video_edit_mode`, `source_*`) and complex exclusion rules, increasing branch surface.
Tests explicitly verify both canonical and legacy paths in the same suite, confirming long-lived dual maintenance burden.
findings.suggestion: Replace field-by-field duplicated type definitions with a shared canonical base type plus a small legacy extension type used only by the adapter parser. Generate canonical input once, then validate once in `joinClips.ts` to eliminate schema drift between wrapper and implementation.

findings.summary: VideoLightbox hooks import types from the component module that imports them back
evidence: `VideoLightbox.tsx` imports `useVideoLightboxRenderModel` and `useVideoLightboxController`.
`useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx` imports `VideoLightboxPropsWithMedia`, `VideoLightboxSharedStateModel`, and `VideoLightboxEditModel` from `../VideoLightbox`.
`useVideoLightboxController.ts` imports `VideoLightboxProps` from `../VideoLightbox`.
This creates a reverse dependency edge from hooks to component module, making the graph cycle-prone and harder to split independently.
findings.suggestion: Extract shared lightbox contracts into a neutral `VideoLightbox.types.ts` (or existing `types.ts`) and have both `VideoLightbox.tsx` and hooks import from that file. Keep component modules dependency-leaf where possible.

findings.summary: Inline editor is tightly coupled to MediaLightbox internal hook bundle
evidence: `useInlineEditState.ts` imports many internals from `@/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks` (`useInpainting`, `useMagicEditMode`, `useRepositionMode`, `useImg2ImgMode`, `useEditSettingsPersistence`, etc.), effectively binding tool behavior to lightbox internals.
Inline edit view composes these states through `ImageEditProvider` and `EditModePanel`, reusing lightbox presentation/state machinery rather than a stable tool-level interface.

ImageLightbox maintains overlapping orchestration responsibilities (`useImageEditOrchestrator`, shared lightbox state, edit-mode persistence), so internal lightbox changes can ripple into the standalone edit-images tool.
findings.suggestion: Define a stable `image-edit-core` hook interface (tool-agnostic domain layer) and have both `ImageLightbox` and `InlineEdit` consume it. Keep UI-shell hooks in MediaLightbox thin wrappers over that core to reduce cross-subsystem dependency blast radius.

findings.summary: Shared `OperationResult` APIs encode failures inconsistently, so callers cannot treat `ok` uniformly across edge helpers.
evidence: `checkRateLimit` returns `operationSuccess(...)` even when rate limiting fails (`allowed: false`) and even when RPC/runtime errors occur (fail-open degraded path).
`parseJsonBody`/`ensureRequestMethod` return `operationFailure(...)` on invalid request shape/method, and `triggerCostCalculation` returns `operationFailure(...)` on invalid downstream payloads.
All of these use the same `OperationResult` type family, but the meaning of `ok` changes by module.
findings.suggestion: Define one explicit contract for `OperationResult`: either (a) `ok=false` always means operation-level failure (including RPC/runtime errors), and domain outcomes stay in `value`; or (b) split into dedicated result types (`RateLimitDecision`, `ParseResult`, etc.) instead of reusing a single generic envelope with divergent semantics.

findings.summary: Rate-limit retry metadata can become negative and leak to response headers/body.
evidence: `checkRateLimit` computes `retryAfter` as `Math.ceil((resetAt.getTime() - now.getTime()) / 1000)` with no lower bound.
`rateLimitResponse` interpolates this value into the message and `Retry-After` header path.
`edgeErrorResponse` serializes `retryAfter` directly when provided.
findings.suggestion: Clamp `retryAfter` to `Math.max(0, ...)` before returning, and only emit `Retry-After`/retry text when value is > 0 to keep response contracts valid under clock skew or stale reset timestamps.

findings.summary: Client-side recoverable operations expose incompatible error channels (boolean, throw, and result object), creating unpredictable call-site handling.
evidence: `safePlay` catches and logs then returns `Promise<boolean>` (error details are dropped to caller).
`fetchToolSettingsSupabase` throws `ToolSettingsError` for many paths, but also returns fallback settings for some timeout/auth-string cases.
`consumePendingJoinClips` and `loadClipDuration` return `OperationResult` with structured error codes for similar recoverable behavior.
findings.suggestion: Adopt one recoverable-operation contract for shared utilities (for example `OperationResult<T>` with standardized `errorCode/recoverable` fields), then provide thin adapters only at UI boundaries where boolean convenience is needed.

findings.summary: `useLineageChain` advertises nullable inputs but internally treats them as non-null, making the public API less honest than implementation requirements.
evidence: `useLineageChain(variantId: string | null, projectId: string | null)` builds `queryKey` and `queryFn` using `variantId!` and `projectId!`.
The downstream traversal path (`resolveVariantProjectScope(variantId, projectId)`) requires concrete IDs, so nullability is only guarded indirectly by `enabled`.
findings.suggestion: Split into two explicit layers: a nullable wrapper that returns an idle state, and a strict internal/query function that accepts only non-null IDs; remove `!` assertions from public-hook internals.

findings.summary: Two deprecated re-export shims have no owner/removal target enforcement, allowing indefinite migration drift.
evidence: `buttonVariants.ts` marks itself deprecated and says "while imports migrate" but only re-exports (`:1-8`) with no `@removalTarget`, policy lookup, or runtime signal.
`toolConstants.ts` has the same pattern (`:1-6`): deprecated compatibility shim text but no governance metadata/enforcement.
Contrast in same batch: other aliases do include governance contracts and enforcement (`src/shared/hooks/useCredits.ts`, `src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade.ts`).
findings.suggestion: Apply the same deprecation contract pattern used elsewhere: add policy-backed owner/removal target metadata and usage signaling, then track import count to drive removal of both shim files.

findings.summary: Join-clips and travel-between-images still expose broad legacy input surfaces, keeping old and new schemas coexisting in core task boundaries.
evidence: `JoinClipsTaskCompatParams` includes many legacy fields (`starting_video_path`, `video_edit_mode`, `source_*`, etc.) across the main adapter input (`joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts:32-95`).
Adapter still actively transforms legacy fields into canonical config (`:160-197`, `:263-301`) and remains exported API (`:369-393`).
`legacyStructureVideo.ts` exports interfaces with multiple deprecated task fields (`:18-46`) and no runtime gating/validation to reject legacy fields when migration date passes.
findings.suggestion: Narrow compatibility to an edge-only normalization layer: keep canonical task creators strict, move legacy parsing into one ingress boundary, and add fail-fast removal gates for deprecated fields once target date is reached.

findings.summary: Multiple helpers silently accept historical field names but provide no migration observability or retirement path.
evidence: `getGenerationId` falls back through `generation_id -> metadata.generation_id -> id` for "various data shapes" (`mediaTypeHelpers.ts:16-34`) without deprecation tracking.
`getSourceTaskId` accepts `source_task_id`, `orchestrator_task_id`, and legacy `task_id` (`taskIdHelpers.ts:13-32`) but has no owner/removal target policy.
`extractSegmentImages` reads both explicit per-segment fields and older orchestrator array fields (`galleryUtils.ts:43-73`) with silent fallback behavior.
findings.suggestion: Introduce versioned param contracts plus telemetry on fallback-path usage; emit warnings/counters when legacy keys are used and schedule deletion of low-usage fallback branches.

findings.summary: Deprecation contract enforcement uses divergent policy sources across modules
evidence: `handleError.ts` pulls policy from centralized governance (`getDeprecationPolicy('runtime_error_alias')`).
`legacySupabaseFacade.ts` bypasses that registry and constructs policy from `legacySupabasePolicy` constants before enforcement.
README presents these as governed compatibility contracts under shared quality gates, but runtime implementation is not structurally uniform.
findings.suggestion: Standardize deprecation policy lookup through one governance API (for example, `getDeprecationPolicy(...)`) for both error and Supabase legacy aliases, and keep module-specific constants only as data inputs to that shared resolver.

findings.summary: UI button entry point blends themed app surface with base primitive exports
evidence: `button.tsx` wraps base primitives and app theme variants, but exports both `Button` and base-layer contracts (`BaseButton`, `baseButtonVariants`) from the same module.
This creates a single high-import hub where consumers can cross layers accidentally, weakening intended boundary direction between primitive and themed surfaces.
findings.suggestion: Split exports into explicit layer entry points (`ui/baseButton` for primitives, `ui/button` for themed app button) and remove base exports from `button.tsx` to enforce dependency direction at import boundaries.

findings.summary: Deprecated aliases remain inside canonical modules, prolonging contract ambiguity
evidence: `handleError.ts` exports both canonical API (`handleError`) and deprecated alias API (`reportRuntimeError`, `RuntimeErrorOptions`) in the same contract file.
`legacySupabaseFacade.ts` remains a runtime callable shim with active importers, while README describes migration gates and a removal target (`2026-06-30`).
Keeping live aliases in primary runtime surfaces increases long-tail compatibility usage and broadens blast radius for future removals.
findings.suggestion: Move deprecated aliases into dedicated legacy-only modules with explicit re-export boundaries, then keep canonical modules alias-free; wire quality gates to fail new imports from those legacy entry points.

findings.summary: Segment deletion path performs generation reads and deletes by raw IDs without enforcing project scope, unlike sibling mutation code.
evidence: useShotImagesEditorCallbacks reads target generation with .eq('id', generationId) and sibling set with .eq('parent_generation_id', beforeData.parent_generation_id), then deletes via .from('generations').delete().in('id', idsToDelete) (lines 65-69, 97-101, 125).
useGenerationMutations consistently requires project scope and applies .eq('project_id', projectId) plus pre-verification before writes (lines 105-113, 126-127, 230-237, 320-321).
findings.suggestion: Route segment deletion through a shared scoped mutation helper: require projectId input, validate ownership (existing resolve/verify helpers), and add project-bound predicates to lookup and delete queries so all generation mutations follow one authorization pattern.

findings.summary: Generation creation skips the project-scope guard pattern used by other generation mutations, relying only on caller-provided projectId.
evidence: createGeneration inserts into generations and generation_variants directly with project_id: params.projectId and no ensureProjectScope/verifyGenerationScope call (useGenerationMutations lines 151-213).
In the same file, update/delete/toggle paths explicitly enforce scope before writes, indicating create is an outlier in authorization handling.

Project scope is propagated via UI state/global context pathways (ProjectContext writes window.__PROJECT_CONTEXT__), increasing dependence on client-side correctness if create path does not apply the same guard discipline.
findings.suggestion: Apply the same pre-write authorization contract in createGeneration as other mutation paths: normalize/validate project scope centrally and verify project accessibility before insert, or move all generation writes behind a shared scoped service/API surface.
